svg.text: Explaining tree-ensemble predictions by finding the training examples that shaped them most

text.tspan: For any prediction, return the K training points that most influenced it —
identified by counting how many leaves they share with the test sample across every tree in the ensemble.

g.text: Drop the test point into every tree.
Each tree routes it to one specific leaf.

text.tspan: Stacked together, the leaf indices form a
fingerprint of how the ensemble routes x

g.text: Encode every training point the same way.
Count how many leaves each one shares with vtest.

text.tspan: sim(xtest, xi) =fraction of trees that route both points
A high score means the ensemble treats x
they share the same regions of the learned feature space.

g.text: Return the top-K most similar training
points. They are the model’s evidence.

text.tspan: inspectableexamples a domain expert can read —
Trees split the feature space into regions — the leaves. Two points landing in the same leaf are treated identically by that tree.
Counting shared leaves across the whole forest yields a similarity that reflects what the model actually attends to, not raw feature distance.
It is interpretable, model-faithful, cheap to compute, and complements SHAP / LIME-style attributions.
